zh.Cubbleston, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾約克
zh.Walton-in-Knaptoft, , Leicestershire, En: 英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾沃爾頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Trussell於1311年出生在英國爾時，她的父親William De Trussell51歲，母親Matilda Mainwaring32歲。她與Sir John St Pierre在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世在英國肯特艾格頓。

zh-hans.Cubbleston, Cheshire, England: 英国英格兰越可爾約克
zh-hans.Walton-in-Knaptoft, , Leicestershire, En: 英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾沃爾頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Trussell于1311年出生在英国爾时，她的父亲William De Trussell51岁，母亲Matilda Mainwaring32岁。她与Sir John St Pierre在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世在英国肯特艾格頓。

nameMeanings.name: Trussell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English trus(s)el(le) ‘bundle, parcel’ (Old French troussel), perhaps for a porter (see Truss ), or from the same word in the sense ‘stamp, mould (for stamping coins)’, for someone who minted coins.
variant of Thrussell, a nickname from Middle English thros(t)el, thrus(t)el ‘song thrush’ (Old English thros(t)le). Compare Thrush .

variants.name: Truss
meanings: English (mainly Essex and Middlesex): metonymic occupational name from Middle English trusse ‘bundle, package’ (Old French trousse), perhaps for a porter. See also Trussell .
Americanized form of Ukrainian Trus: nickname from trus ‘rabbit’, typically applied to someone thought to be a coward.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Trussell was born in 1311, in Cheshire, England, her father, William De Trussell, was 51 and her mother, Matilda Mainwaring, was 32. She married Sir John St Pierre in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in Egerton, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Trussell est née en 1311, à Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, William De Trussell, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Matilda Mainwaring, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John St Pierre à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée à Egerton, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Trussell 1311 in Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William De Trussell 51 und ihre Mutter Matilda Mainwaring 32. Sie hat in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Sir John St Pierre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb in Egerton, Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Trussell è nata il nel 1311 nel Cheshire, England, sua padre, William De Trussell, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Matilda Mainwaring, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Sir John St Pierre nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel Egerton, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Trussellが1311年にイギリスチェシュアイアで生まれた時，父William De Trussellは51歳，母Matilda Mainwaringは32歳であった。イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでSir John St Pierreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスケントエジャートンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Trussell(이)가 1311년 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William De Trussell(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda Mainwaring(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Sir John St Pierre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 켄트 거턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Trussell nasceu em 1311, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William De Trussell, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Mainwaring, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John St Pierre em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em Egerton, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Trussell родилась в 1311 г. в местности чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени William De Trussell было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Matilda Mainwaring, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John St Pierre в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в местности эгиртон, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Trussell nació en 1311, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William De Trussell, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Matilda Mainwaring, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Sir John St Pierre en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en Egerton, Kent, Inglaterra.
